content.full_text:
Free ticket to derivaixer -
repotirs reguirements.

DANIEL VAN VOORHIS DVAN VOORHIS & SKAGGS 82015 04/2/0
SANFORD M. SKAGGS ATTORNEYS AT LAW 203/2
FLAGG PLAZA - SUITE 111
TIMOTHY M. McMAHON 1855 OLYMPIC BOULEVARD TELEPHONE
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 94596 (415) 937-8000

March 2, 1976

A-46 $75

Mr. Ted Prim
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Citizens for Nejedly

Dear Ted:

This will confirm our telephone conversation of February 6,
1976 and your telephone message of March 1.

I called to inquire whether recipients of complimentary
tickets to Senator Nejedly's fundraising dinner would be
required to report such as gifts. The recipients were each
office holders in their own right and therefore required to
file annual disclosure statements, etc. Based upon an
opinion previously rendered by the FPPC to Controller Cory,
you concluded that the recipients of the tickets did not
have to report the receipt of such on the basis that they
had given fair value therefor by attending the event and
lending their political support to the Senator.

During our conversation the question was raised whether the
Senator's committee would have to report the gift of the
tickets to those officials. If so, then the question was
asked whether the gift would be the value of the dinner or
the face amount of the ticket which was substantially in
excess of the former. Your telephone message, left with my
secretary, indicates that the Senator's committee need not
report the gift of those tickets in its campaign statements.

I appreciate your help in these matter.

Very truly yours,

Sanford M. Skaggs
(415) 937-7600

sections.question_synthetic: Whether recipients of complimentary tickets to a fundraising dinner must report them as gifts, and whether the Senator's committee must report the gift of tickets in campaign statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is incoming correspondence/letter from attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. Contains discussion of FPPC advice previously rendered to Controller Cory and telephone conclusions reached, but is formatted as attorney confirmation letter rather than formal FPPC response. Poor document quality and informal formatting make extraction challenging.

embedding.summary: This is correspondence from an attorney (Sanford Skaggs) to Ted Prim at the FPPC confirming telephone conversations regarding gift reporting requirements for complimentary fundraising dinner tickets received by elected officials.
